outcome: Motion approved unanimously; question placed on June 18, 2019 Special Election ballot.
evidence_quote: Shall the Town of Marblehead be allowed to exempt from the provisions of proposition two and one-half, so-called, the amounts required to pay for the bond issued for the purpose of funding the design, project management and construction, equipping and outfitting of a new Pre-K through Third Grade Elementary School with an approximate square footage of 81,935 square feet located at the Bell School site, located at 40 Baldwin Road, which shall have an anticipated useful life as an educational facility for the instruction of school children for at least 50 years all as approved under Article 51 of the 2019 Annual Town Meeting?

what_was_tried: Committee discussed the Powers and Sullivan audit findings regarding FY18 invoices being paid in FY19, and committed to contacting the State Bureau of Accounts for guidance on internal controls and to modify reporting procedures affecting school budget and administration.
outcome: Schaeffner to set up a call with the State Bureau of Accounts and report back on remedy; awaiting State ruling.
evidence_quote: Ms. Schaeffner stated that she will ask the State Bureau of Accounts for ideas on internal controls. She said that they will get guidance on how to modify reporting and will set up a call and invite the SC to attend and report back on the remedy.

outcome: Motion passed unanimously 5-0; remaining $531,411 deficit unaddressed.
evidence_quote: Ms. Schaeffner made motion to approve the transfers totaling $200,000 as presented. Mr. Harris seconded the motion. Ms. Tedford asked for a vote for the motion on the table to approve transfers of $200,000.00. The Committee VOTED unanimously, 5-0, in favor.

what_was_tried: Reported on a National Grid/Standard Electric LED upgrade project at the High School financed at 0% interest over four years, with electrical savings exceeding monthly payments, making the project funding-neutral and yielding savings after payoff.
outcome: Project completed; immediate savings reported.
evidence_quote: The NG and Standard Electric came to us with a proposal to fund this for four years at 0% interest and the electrical savings is more than the monthly payment to pay for this project. We will see savings immediately this year and once the project is paid off the savings will go much higher.

what_was_tried: Authorized RDA and Leftfield to submit the revised Preliminary School Building Committee Project Scope and Budget Report (PSR) to the MSBA, including a space study at 82,331 GSF, construction value estimated at $44.37m, and total project budget of $56.34m, with CM-R as the preferred construction procurement method.
outcome: Motion approved unanimously by Building Committee members present, 17-0-4-0.
evidence_quote: Ms. Martin made a motion to approve RDA and Leftfield, on behalf of the Building Committee, to submit the revised PSR to the MSBA for review and consideration. Items to specifically note include a red-lined education plan, space study at 82,331 GSF, construction value estimated at $44.37m, total project budget of $56.34m., plan approved as L-shaped, flat roof design, displacement air with full air conditioning, site modifications made in response to MSBA commentary, utilization of CM-R as the preferred method of construction procurement, and a facility and student capacity analysis as requested by the MSBA.

what_was_tried: Justified retaining 2,825 sqft of SPED space above MSBA guidelines (distributed throughout the building, including language-based room, academic skills room, resource room, OT/PT, testing room, two de-escalation rooms, SPED conference room, and coordinator/secretary offices), with the rationale that without these spaces students would be outsourced to programs outside Marblehead at prohibitive expense.
outcome: SPED space retained in the PSR submission at 82,331 GSF; rationale to be included in September 12th MSBA submission.
evidence_quote: She noted that if these spaces are not implemented into the new building then students will be outsourced to programs outside of Marblehead, which she explained was both against the community's desire to educate all children locally and also prohibitively expensive.

what_was_tried: Discussed the SPED square footage in the Bell School project (2,825 sq ft over MSBA guidelines) and agreed to submit a response to MSBA documenting that relocated collaborative rooms, PreK moving from Bell to Glover, and increased SPED demand system-wide justify the additional SPED space in the new 450-student building, including preparing a redlined educational plan and capacity explanation for the final PSR.
outcome: No vote; agreed that Ms. Perry would prepare the redline space plan and capacity explanation for submission with the final PSR to MSBA by September 12th, with approval to be sought at a September 6th joint meeting.
evidence_quote: She reiterated that there is no extra space within the existing schools and that the new 450 student building, complete with the SPED rooms above the MSBA template, is required for the effective and proper delivery of education and that the response to the MSBA will look to clearly explain and confirm this.
